title: Rafa's Orlando Plan — Financial English
description: Business and financial vocabulary for opening a restaurant in the US. Rafa sits with a US accountant and drowns in margin, runway, cash flow, and ROI.
summary_pt_br: Inglês financeiro/empresarial para o Rafa abrir o Tropical Bites em Orlando. Vocabulário essencial: revenue, margin, cash flow, runway, burn rate, equity, investor, LLC, break-even. Expressões comuns em reuniões: ballpark, run the numbers, bootstrap, in the red/black.

scenes.text_en:
Rafa Zoomed in with an accountant in Florida.
'Let's run the NUMBERS,' the accountant said.
'Your REVENUE looks decent. But what's your MARGIN?
CASH FLOW? RUNWAY? Are you going to BOOTSTRAP, or do you have an INVESTOR?'
Rafa scribbled every word.
By the end, he had a notebook full of English and a ten-year plan.
scenes.text_pt:
O Rafa entrou numa Zoom com um contador da Flórida.
'Vamos rodar os NÚMEROS,' o contador disse.
'RECEITA razoável. Mas e a MARGEM?
FLUXO DE CAIXA? PISTA? Vai FAZER SOZINHO ou tem um INVESTIDOR?'
O Rafa anotava cada palavra.
No fim, tinha um caderno cheio de inglês e um plano de dez anos.

items.pt: fluxo de caixa (dinheiro entrando e saindo)
ritmo de queima de caixa por mês
ponto de equilíbrio (nem lucro nem prejuízo)

items.term: in the black / in the red

items.pt: rodar os números, fazer as contas
empreender sem investidor, com dinheiro próprio
tipo de empresa nos EUA, similar a LTDA

scenes.title: Formal hedging — softening in business
scenes.body_en: Financial conversations avoid absolutes. Use hedges: 'TENDS to be around 20%', 'ROUGHLY SPEAKING', 'A BALLPARK figure would be $500k', 'I'D ESTIMATE...'. This protects you from being wrong and signals professional caution.
scenes.body_pt: Conversas financeiras evitam absolutos. Hedges (amortecedores): 'tends to be', 'roughly speaking', 'a ballpark figure', 'I'd estimate'. Protege de erro e mostra cautela profissional.

examples.en: Our margin tends to be around 25%.
examples.pt: Nossa margem costuma ser em torno de 25%.

examples.en: Roughly speaking, we break even at 200 tacos a day.
examples.pt: Grosso modo, batemos o break-even com 200 tacos/dia.

examples.en: A ballpark figure would be $40k in startup costs.
examples.pt: Uma estimativa seria uns US$40k de custo inicial.

exercise.question: 'We're ___ — we're spending more than we earn.' Which idiom?
exercise.explanation: IN THE RED = losing money. IN THE BLACK = profitable. Finance color-codes: red for negative.
exercise.hint_pt_br: IN THE RED = no prejuízo. IN THE BLACK = no lucro.

exercise.question: Complete: 'What's our ___ ___? We need 18 months to survive.' (pista, tempo até o caixa acabar)
exercise.explanation: RUNWAY = how long your cash lasts at current burn rate. Airport metaphor: how much runway before takeoff (or crash).
exercise.hint_pt_br: RUNWAY = tempo até o caixa acabar. Metáfora de aeroporto.

exercise.explanation: Six essential nouns. If you can't use them in a sentence, you can't talk business in English.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Seis substantivos fundamentais. Se não souber usar em frase, não dá pra conversar negócios em inglês.

scenes.location_en: Zoom, final minutes of the meeting

turns.en: So roughly speaking, what's my break-even?
turns.pt: Grosso modo, qual é meu break-even?

turns.en: Based on your numbers? 220 tacos a day. That's the ballpark.
turns.pt: Pelos seus números? 220 tacos por dia. É a estimativa.

turns.en: And if I bootstrap, how long is my runway?
turns.pt: E se eu fizer sem investidor, qual é minha pista?

turns.en: About 14 months. After that, you need revenue — or an investor.
turns.pt: Uns 14 meses. Depois, precisa de receita — ou de um investidor.

exercise.question: 'We're bootstrapping the business.' What does that mean?
options: We're funding it ourselves, no investors
We're going public on the stock market
exercise.explanation: BOOTSTRAP = build the business with your own money, no outside investment. From 'pull yourself up by your bootstraps'.
exercise.hint_pt_br: BOOTSTRAP = fazer com dinheiro próprio, sem investidor. Vem de 'se virar sozinho'.
